question.text: An importer who needs to pay USD should hedge by:

answer.text: Buying USDINR futures or buying USDINR call options
Selling USDINR futures or buying USDINR put options

explanation.text: **The Logic:** An importer paying in USD faces the risk of USD appreciation against INR (USDINR going up). To hedge: buy USDINR futures (locks in the buying rate) or buy USDINR calls (insurance against USD rise). **The Trap:** Selling call options would give limited income but UNLIMITED risk if USD rises — not a good hedge. **Cross-Exam Context:** Import hedging is tested in Series I. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The contract month should match the expected payment date (close to but before the futures expiry).
